title: Family Beach Day: Water Safety
description: A morning of games and hands-on water-safety practice with park staff. Life-jacket fittings for kids, rip-current basics, and free sunscreen while it lasts.

title: Owl Prowl at the Nature Center
description: Join a naturalist for a guided evening walk listening for barred and great horned owls, then meet an education raptor up close. Ages 8 and up; bring a flashlight.

title: Campground Campfire: Stories & S'mores
description: An easy evening at the group fire ring — tall tales from park rangers, s'mores supplies provided, and tips for your next campout. Open to campers and day visitors.

title: One-Room School Day at Mt. Gilead
description: Experience an 1880s school morning — McGuffey's readers, slate penmanship, and recess games — led by a teacher in period costume. Seats are limited to 30.

title: Sunset Paddle at Paradise Pointe
description: A guided flat-water paddle from the marina cove into open water for sunset. Kayaks and PFDs provided, or bring your own. Beginner friendly.

title: Lantern Tours of the James Farm
description: Walk the farm by lantern light with costumed interpreters telling the story of the James family, the war that shaped them, and the night the Pinkertons came.

title: Smithville Open Disc Golf Tournament
description: Amateur and open divisions across the three 18-hole courses at Little Platte Park. Player pack included with registration; spectators free.

title: "The Liberty Raid: 1866" — History Talk
description: A curator-led look at the nation's first successful daylight peacetime bank robbery — what we know, what we don't, and why it was never solved.

title: Monarch Tagging Workshop
description: Help net, tag, and release migrating monarchs for citizen science. All materials provided; great for families and scout groups.

title: Harvest Day at Pharis Farm
description: The 1927 farmstead opens for the season's harvest — antique equipment demonstrations, cider pressing, and conservation stories from the Pharis legacy.
